input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest .NET SDK versions
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install (e.g., .NET 8 or 9)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find exact names
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios, maccatalyst sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows targeting requires the Windows SDK and UWP/WinUI build tools:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --silent `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK `
              --includeRecommended --quiet --wait"
```

## 4. Install Java JDK (Android build requirement)

```powershell
# Discover available versions
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install JDK 17 (required by Android Gradle)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --silent

# Verify
java --version
```

## 5. Install Android SDK & Emulator

```powershell
# Option A: Let MAUI handle it (auto-provisioned on first build)
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net8.0-android `
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" `
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true

# Option B: Manual via command-line tools
# Download cmdline-tools from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools
# Then:
$sdkRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
$sdkManager = "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# Discover available platforms/build-tools
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "build-tools;"

# Install required components (use latest discovered versions)
& $sdkManager "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" "emulator" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"
```

## 6. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17", "User")

# Refresh current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
$env:JAVA_HOME = "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17"
```

## 7. Create an Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" -d "pixel"
```

## 8. Verify Everything

```powershell
# Create and build a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest -o ./MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

---

**Key discovery commands recap:**
| What | Command |
|------|---------|
| .NET SDKs available | `winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK` |
| Installed SDKs | `dotnet --list-sdks` |
| MAUI workloads | `dotnet workload search maui` |
| Android platforms | `sdkmanager --list` |
| Target frameworks | `dotnet new maui --list` then inspect `.csproj` |

Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if you install .NET 9. The `--list` flags let you dynamically find the latest versions rather than hardcoding them.
